title: The Yahrzeit: Remembering Loved Ones Each Year
title_zh: 亚兹海特：每年纪念逝去的亲人
title_he: יום השנה (יארצייט): זכר לאהובינו שעברו לעולמם
description: An educational introduction to the Jewish practice of yahrzeit - the annual commemoration of a loved one's death through candle lighting, Kaddish prayer, Torah study, and charitable giving.
description_zh: 关于犹太传统中亚兹海特习俗的教育性介绍——通过点蜡烛、诵读卡迪什祷文、学习妥拉和慈善捐赠来每年纪念逝者。
description_he: מבוא חינוכי לנוהג היארצייט היהודי - הנצחת שנתית של פטירת אדם אהוב באמצעות הדלקת נר, אמירת קדיש, לימוד תורה ונתינת צדקה.
key_concepts: Annual memorial observance on Hebrew calendar date of death
Torah study and charitable giving in memory of deceased
Elevating the soul through good deeds
target_audience: Chinese native Mandarin speakers with no prior Jewish knowledge
sources: Goldberg, Chaim Binyamin. Mourning in Halachah. Mesorah Publications, 1991
Lamm, Maurice. The Jewish Way in Death and Mourning. Jonathan David Publishers, 2000
related_articles: Shiva: The Seven Days of Mourning
Jewish Views on Death and the Afterlife
Visiting Jewish Cemeteries: Customs and Etiquette
chinese_cultural_notes: This article draws extensive parallels between yahrzeit and Chinese Qingming Festival, Japanese Obon, and broader ancestor veneration traditions. Emphasizes the universal human need to honor the deceased and maintain connection with those who have passed. Highlights similarities in practices: visiting graves, making offerings, lighting candles/incense, and belief that actions of the living benefit the deceased.
